docs.title: Repo Template
docs.text:
A clean template to kickstart your deep learning project 🚀⚡🔥 Click on Use this template to initialize new repository.

Suggestions are always welcome!

docs.title: Description
docs.text: This is a template for you to use for any project. It has all the actions set up for you to use.

docs.title: DocsGenerator
docs.text:
Bases: BaseModel

DocsGenerator is a class that generates documentation for Python files or classes within a specified source directory.

Attributes:

Name Type Description source str

The source directory or file path.

output str

The output directory path.

exclude str

Comma-separated list of folders or files to exclude.

mode Literal['file', 'class']

Mode of documentation generation, either by file or class.

Methods:

Name Description gen_docs

Generates documentation by file or class.

__call__

Asynchronously calls the gen_docs method.

Using CLI 
python ./scripts/gen_docs.py --source ./src --output ./docs/Reference --exclude .venv gen_docs
Using Rye 
uv run python ./scripts/gen_docs.py

docs.title: source_path
docs.text:
source_path: Path = Field(
    ...,
    title="The Source File Path or Folder Path",
    description="This field can be a file path or folder path, if it is a folder path, it will automatically search for python and ipynb files.",
    examples=["./src"],
    alias="source",
    frozen=True,
)

docs.title: output_path
docs.text:
output_path: Path = Field(
    ...,
    title="The Output Path",
    description="The output path for the generated documentation.",
    examples=["./docs/Reference"],
    alias="output",
    frozen=True,
)

docs.title: exclude
docs.text:
exclude: str = Field(
    default=".venv",
    description="Exclude the folder or file, it should be separated by comma.",
    examples=[".venv,.git,.idea"],
)

docs.title: mode
docs.text:
mode: Literal["file", "class"] = Field(
    default="class",
    title="The Document Style",
    description="Generate docs by file or class.",
    examples=["file", "class"],
)

docs.title: source_files
docs.text:
source_files: list[Path]

Computed property that returns the source path as a Path object.

Returns:

Name Type Description Path list[Path]

The source path.

docs.title: gen_docs
docs.text:
gen_docs() -> None
Source code in scripts/gen_docs.py 
async def gen_docs(self) -> None:
    with Progress() as progress:
        task = progress.add_task("[green]Generating docs...")

        progress.update(
            task_id=task, description="[cyan]Files Found...", total=len(self.source_files)
        )

        for source_file in self.source_files:
            progress.update(
                task_id=task,
                advance=1,
                description=f"[cyan]Processing {source_file.as_posix()}...",
                refresh=True,
            )
            if source_file.suffix == ".ipynb":
                await self.__gen_notebook_docs(file=source_file)
            elif source_file.suffix == ".py":
                await self.__gen_python_docs(file=source_file)
            else:
                console.log(f"Unsupported file type: {source_file.suffix}")

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This page will guide you through the installation process this project.

docs.title: Install Dependency Management Tool
docs.text:
uvRyeCondaPIP

Install uv

make uv-install

Install Rye

make rye-install
  • Please visit miniconda to install miniconda.
conda create -n plotly python=3.9 -y
conda activate plotly
pip install uv
  • Please visit Python to install Python for using pip.
pip install uv

docs.title: Install/Setup Dependencies
docs.text:
uvRyeCondaPIP 
uv sync

rye sync

uv pip sync pyproject.toml

uv pip sync pyproject.toml
  • Once you have done the above steps, you can run the following commands to start the application.
  • If your environment is messed up, you can run uv sync again to fix it.
